data.title: Clarion Partners invests $2 billion in industrial acquisitions, developing 5.5M sq ft properties globally.
data.description: A prominent real estate investment firm, Clarion Partners, has been actively growing its industrial and logistics presence in the U.S. and Europe. Ranking as the third-largest industrial real estate manager in the U.S., the company has recently completed over $2 billion in gross industrial acquisitions in the past year alone. Additionally, within this timeframe, Clarion commenced the development of around 5.5 million square feet of state-of-the-art, Class A industrial properties. This strategic expansion showcases Clarion's commitment to enhancing its industrial and logistics platform across key markets.

data.title: Halper Sadeh LLC urges ONTF, SNDA, ISPO shareholders to act quickly and contact the firm to discuss legal rights.
data.description: Investors are advised to reach out to the company promptly as time to assert their rights might be limited. A legal firm, Halper Sadeh LLC in New York, reported on Jan. 26, 2026, that they are probing various companies for potential infringements of federal securities laws or breaches of conduct.

data.title: Investors in DHIL, UDMY, DXLG urged to contact Halper Sadeh LLC for breach discussion. Time may be limited.
data.description: Investors are advised to reach out to the company promptly, as there may be a restricted window to uphold their rights. In a press release dated January 26, 2026, Halper Sadeh LLC, a legal firm specializing in investor rights, disclosed that they are probing into various companies for possible infractions of federal securities regulations and/or breaches of legal duties. The investigation aims to ascertain any discrepancies that may have occurred and provide assistance to concerned stakeholders during this period.

data.title: JamLoop appoints Brian Bell as SVP, Head of Sales, amid CTV industry changes. Brings performance ads to streaming TV.
data.description: In Walnut Creek, California on January 26, 2026, JamLoop revealed the acquisition of Brian Bell as the new Senior Vice President and Head of Sales. The tech platform, known for prioritizing connected TV in performance advertising for streaming TV, made this move during a pivotal period for the sector.

data.title: Investor rights law firm Halper Sadeh LLC is investigating WAT, TWO, UBFO, ALEX for violations. Contact firm for rights.
data.description: Investors are advised to reach out to the company promptly as time may be running out to take action on their rights. On January 26, 2026, in New York, Halper Sadeh LLC, a legal firm focusing on protecting investor rights, disclosed investigations into multiple companies for potential infringements of federal securities laws and breaches. Shareholders are urged to be proactive due to the time-sensitive nature of these concerns.

data.title: Tradr to launch 200% long exposure leveraged ETFs on LITE, SNDK, and WDC - first-to-market singles.
data.description: Three innovative single-stock leveraged ETFs offering 200% long exposure are set to be introduced by Tradr ETFs, a company specializing in ETFs for experienced investors and professional traders. The launch of these ETFs marks a significant development in the market, providing investors with unique opportunities to capitalize on market movements.

data.title: Natural gas prices surge by 30% due to Winter Storm Fern, Henry Hub contract rises above $6 per million BTUs. Silver jumps 13% to $116/ounce, with a 266% 12-month gain. Gold rises 2.2% to $5,100. Japan yen strengthens, dollar weakens. U.S. equities rise.
data.description: U.S. natural gas prices surged over 30% due to Winter Storm Fern, driving heating demand and disrupting supply, resulting in the largest weekly percentage gain ever for a Nymex front-month contract. The Henry Hub front-month contract is now trading above $6 per million British thermal units, a 125% surge from $3 a week ago. Cloudflare stock saw gains and there was a broader commodity breakout due to dollar weakness. Gold rose 2.2% to $5,100, while silver surged 13% to $116 per ounce, its best one-day surge since September 2008. The rolling 12-month gain for silver is now at 266%, the strongest since 1980. The dollar remained under pressure, which led to a rise in Japanese yen and suspicions of currency intervention by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. U.S. equities continued to rise.

data.title: New report on EV integration trends reveals installation of innovative technologies like three-motor four-wheel drive, drive-brake integration & corner modules.
data.description: Dublin - There is a new report available titled "New Energy Vehicle Cross-Domain (Electric Drive System and Powertrain Domain) Integration Trend Report" for 2025-2026. The report highlights key insights on advancements in electric drive and powertrain domain research. Technologies like three-motor four-wheel drive, drive-brake integration, and corner modules are gaining traction in vehicles. The integration of electric drive is progressing towards powertrain domain and cross-domain integration. This signifies a shift towards innovation and efficiency in the automotive sector.

data.title: Krispy Kreme brings back fan-favorite Chocomania Collection with 4 new chocolate-glazed doughnuts. Available Jan 27 - Feb 1.
data.description: Charlotte, N.C.-- Krispy Kreme® is bringing back Chocomania with a twist, offering four new chocolate-glazed doughnuts featuring HERSHEY’S chocolate. The limited-time Chocomania Collection will be available at select Krispy Kreme locations nationwide for just six days, running from Tuesday, Jan. 27 to Sunday, Feb. 1. The collection includes: a new Chocolate Cheesecake Doughnut, HERSHEY’S Chocolate Original Glazed® doughnut with cheesecake swirl and HERSHEY’S mini chocolate chips.

data.title: Trade risks rise as Canada-China deal fears reignite, impacting earnings for mega caps. US tariff concerns loom, rekindling sensitivity in markets, even incremental headlines are enough to reprice risk. Bitcoin declines to 86k as Yen strengthens.
data.description: Risk in trade increases as earnings for mega-cap companies approach, with concerns over potential U.S. tariffs growing due to uncertainties around a Canada-China deal. Although not confirmed policy, markets react strongly to any news reopening the trade front, especially as growth expectations stabilize. Even small updates can impact risk in globally exposed sectors, necessitating quick adjustments in equities, currencies, and interest rates. This headline risk affects market sentiment, dampening short-term enthusiasm. Bitcoin has fallen to around 86k from a recent high of 96k, aligning with a broader downtrend, coinciding with the strengthening of the Yen. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Year-end report for financial year 2025 by SciBase postponed to March 13, 2026, from original date.
data.description: The Board of Directors of SciBase Holding AB (publ) has decided to delay the release of the year-end report for the financial year 2025 until March 13, 2026, due to the need to synchronize trading schedules. This decision was made in Stockholm on January 26, 2026.
